reasoning: Candidate (right, 4x): pixel-art armored figure in blue-white with silver hair. Art quality is clean and competent, pixel style is broadly consistent with retro Nintendo aesthetics. However, the color temperature is distinctly cool (desaturated blue + grey-white) rather than the warm, sunny, Hyrule-golden palette the target tone demands. The armor silhouette reads as a competent generic-fantasy RPG knight rather than a charming toy-diorama character; it lacks the rounded whimsy, warm highlight rims, and expressive chibi-leaning proportions that signal 'wholesome Nintendo family-friendly.' Not failing outright — no grimdark elements, no edginess, no fan-service — but the warmth deficit and generic-knight read push it out of a clean pass. Peer left references include similarly cool-toned helmets, so the candidate is consistent with those peers, but that consistency does not rescue either from the tonal drift. Marginal: needs warmer palette and softer, more character-forward design pass.
